title: Germany vs Paraguay - Post-match analysis
summary: Paraguay advance to face the winner of France vs Sweden. Germany's 2026 World Cup ends here - their earliest exit since 2018, and arguably the most painful in their entire tournament history.

title: Brazil vs Japan - Pre-match preview
summary: Brazil's first real test of the tournament — Japan are unbeaten in 10 games, including a 3-2 comeback win over Brazil last October. Vinicius Jr has scored in every group game, matching a record only Jairzinho, Romario, Ronaldo and Rivaldo have managed — each time Brazil won the title.

title: Algeria vs Austria - A World Cup classic
summary: Six goals, a stoppage-time twist, and a result that eliminated Iran from the best-third-place race entirely. Mahrez scored his first-ever World Cup goals at 35, only for substitute Sasa Kalajdzic to snatch a 61-second equalizer that sent both sides through.

title: Jordan vs Argentina - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Argentina complete a perfect group stage 1-3 without needing Messi to start - Lo Celso's free-kick and Lautaro's penalty did the early damage, before Messi came off the bench to seal it with a record-breaking free-kick of his own, his 7th straight World Cup game scoring.

title: Colombia vs Portugal - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Colombia's first-ever World Cup goalless draw secures top spot in Group K - but the real drama was a stoppage-time Davinson Sanchez header, ruled out by VAR for offside, after Diogo Costa produced a goalkeeping masterclass against 24 Colombian shots.

title: Uruguay vs Spain - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Uruguay 0-1 Spain in a tense, cagey decider settled by a single Muslera blunder. Baena punished the goalkeeper's misjudged clearance just before half-time, Spain's well-organized defending did the rest, and Uruguay are eliminated for the second straight World Cup.

title: Norway vs France - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Norway 1-4 France as Ousmane Dembele scored the second-fastest hat-trick in World Cup history, all inside the first half-hour. The hyped Haaland-Mbappe showdown never materialized — Haaland was rested — but Dembele made this his night entirely with a perfect 10.0 Sofascore rating.

title: Germany vs Ecuador - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: A genuine shock — Ecuador fell behind in the 2nd minute, hadn't scored all tournament before kickoff, and still came from behind to beat Germany 2-1. Angulo's exquisite equalizer and Plata's 77th-minute winner send Ecuador to the knockouts for the first time since 2006.

title: Morocco vs Haiti - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Morocco 4-2 in a chaotic first half that saw Haiti lead twice, before Morocco's greater quality took over. Saibari's equalizer on the stroke of half-time was the turning point, with Rahimi and Yassine sealing it in the second half. Morocco advance to the Round of 32.

title: Scotland vs Brazil - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Brazil 3-0, exactly as the pre-match read suggested. Vinicius Jr brace, Cunha on top, Guimaraes with two assists. Scotland's flat back four problem flagged before kickoff was exposed inside the first minute.

title: England vs Ghana - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: England 0-0 Ghana in a frustrating night — 15 shots, no goals, and two separate refereeing incidents that went England's way, both flagged afterward as wrong. Kane blazed over an open goal late on to sum up the evening.

title: Argentina vs Austria - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Messi is now the outright all-time leading World Cup scorer with 18 goals, surpassing Klose. He missed a penalty then responded with a brace — the second in stoppage time to seal the record. On the 40th anniversary of Maradona's wonder goal.

title: Spain vs Saudi Arabia - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Spain 4-0 in the perfect response to the Cape Verde shock — three goals inside 25 minutes from Yamal and Oyarzabal, who registered three goal involvements in that spell, only the third player ever to do so in World Cup history.

title: Brazil vs Haiti - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Brazil 3-0 with a Cunha brace and Vinicius finish, all before half-time. Ancelotti's decision to start Cunha after his cameo against Morocco was vindicated emphatically — Haiti become the first side eliminated from the tournament.

title: Portugal vs DR Congo - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Portugal 1-1 DR Congo — Wissa's stoppage-time header gave DR Congo their first-ever World Cup goal, 52 years after their only previous appearance. Ronaldo, the oldest outfield player to ever start a World Cup match at 41, missed two big chances.

title: Iraq vs Norway - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Haaland's long-awaited World Cup debut delivered a brace in a 4-1 Norway win. Iraq were competitive and equalized through a brilliant Hussein header, before a goalkeeping error gifted Haaland his second and Norway pulled away.

title: France vs Senegal - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: France 3-1 after a dire first half, then total transformation. Mbappe broke two records in one night — France's all-time scorer (58 goals) and equaling Fontaine's French World Cup record (14). Olise's half-time positional switch was the tactical fix that unlocked everything.

title: Argentina vs Algeria - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Argentina 3-0, but the result is secondary. Messi's first-ever World Cup hat-trick on his 200th cap, exactly 20 years after his debut, equaled Klose's all-time scoring record of 16. There is no version of this analysis that isn't about him.

title: Spain vs Cape Verde - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: One of the biggest shocks in World Cup history — Spain 0-0 Cape Verde. The reigning European champions had 27 shots and couldn't score, thanks to 40-year-old goalkeeper Vozinha producing a perfect 7-save performance on the biggest stage of his career.

title: Germany vs Curacao - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Germany 7-1 in an emphatic opener, but the headline belonged to Curacao — the smallest nation in World Cup history scored their first-ever World Cup goal through Comenencia, sending their fans into delirium even as the scoreline turned heavy.

title: Brazil vs Morocco - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Brazil 1-1 Morocco in a genuinely even contest. Saibari chipped Alisson after 21 minutes, Vinicius Jr struck back with individual brilliance on his 50th cap, and Alisson's stoppage-time double save prevented a Moroccan upset. Saibari registered 100 pressures — the tournament's joint highest.

title: Mexico vs South Africa - Post-match: prediction vs reality
summary: Mexico 2-0 in a chaotic World Cup opener — three red cards, a 2010 rematch, and Raul Jimenez's first ever World Cup goal. Quinones punished a Sithole error after 9 minutes, Jimenez headed in the second, and the match descended into chaos from there.
